title: Bargaining in Markets
description: Bargaining is expected and welcomed in traditional markets. Shops have flexible pricing and expect negotiation.
dos: Start bargaining at 60-70% of the asking price
Bargain only if you're serious about buying
donts: Don't insult the quality of goods
Don't expect fixed-price retailers to negotiate

title: Dress Modestly in Traditional Areas
description: In traditional neighborhoods and religious sites, dressing modestly is expected. Bare shoulders, short skirts are frowned upon.
dos: Cover shoulders and knees in traditional areas
Wear full pants or long skirt
donts: Don't wear sleeveless tops in markets
Don't wear short skirts/shorts in conservative areas
Don't wear revealing clothing in temples

title: Strong Vegetarian Culture
description: About 40-50% of Ahmedabad's population is vegetarian (Jain, Hindu). Vegetarian food is widely available and respected.
dos: Visit vegetarian restaurants for authentic cuisine
donts: Don't assume all Indians eat meat
Don't serve non-vegetarian food to vegetarians without asking

title: Flexible Punctuality (IST - Indian Standard Time)
description: Meetings and social events often start 15-30 minutes late. Flexible attitude to time is normal.
dos: Arrive 10-15 minutes early for important meetings
Don't be upset if others arrive late
donts: Don't arrive exactly on time for social events
Don't complain if things start late

title: Public Spitting - Taboo & Fined
description: Public spitting is illegal and heavily fined. Much stricter enforcement than in smaller cities.

title: Alcohol Prohibition (Dry State)
description: Gujarat is a dry state — sale and public consumption of alcohol is illegal. Visitors need a permit to legally possess alcohol, and bars/liquor stores don't exist the way they do elsewhere in India.
dos: Apply for a tourist liquor permit at designated counters if you need one
Drink only in licensed permit rooms at certain hotels
Respect the prohibition even if it feels unusual
donts: Don't carry alcohol across the state border without a permit
Don't expect to find bars, pubs or liquor stores
Don't get visibly intoxicated in public
